page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, this server allocates dedicated CPU resources with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is consistently positioned in the bottom 10%, as evidenced by stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, placing in the middle 50% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached reads and writes also performs within the average tier.

The inclusion of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance class qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed temporary storage without the overhead of network-attached volumes. It is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive machine learning workloads, as demonstrated by weak performance in medium-sized LLM inference benchmarks and the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. While single-core benchmarks place in the bottom 10%, its multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. The bundled high-speed local storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense applications. This instance is suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks that benefit from fast local temporary storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC processor, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7571 processor at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of DDR4 RAM and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for general-purpose applications, databases, and static web serving
